rulings.Case Title: LOUIS GAMACHE et al VS. NICK VOLENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Anmd Otion To Compel Full And Complete Responses To Requiest For Melvin Munoz Moran To Produce Documents At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 16, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFFS LOUIS GAMACHE AND DANIELLE MCGEE'S Notice Of Motion Anmd Motion To Compel Full And Complete Responses To Requiest For Melvin Munoz Moran To Produce Documents At Deposition. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE GUERRERO VS. WILEY C. VICKREY, ADMINISTRATIVE DIRECTOR OF THE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 16, 2012, Line 3. RESPONDENT WILLIAM VICKREY, THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA AND THE ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE OF THE COURTS' DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Sustained without leave to amend. Cal. Gov't. Code § 71802 does not confer an entitlement to a permanent opt-out status and Plaintiff fails to plead facts showing there was another rule or understanding conferring such entitlement. There is no reasonable probability that granting leave to amend would cure the defects. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. ALL CONTESTED MATTERS WILL BE HEARD AT 10:30AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. UNCONTESTED MATTERS WILL PROCEED AS USUAL AT 9:30AM. = (302/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: MUKUL BAKSHI VS. WORLD AFFAIRS COUNCIL OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 16, 2012, Line 4. DEFENDANT WORLD AFFAIRS COUNCIL OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend. The Court previously struck those allegations per the November 15, 2011 order and the allegations add nothing to the complaint given that plaintiff is not seeking punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. ALL CONTESTED MATTERS WILL BE HEARD AT 10:30AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. UNCONTESTED MATTERS WILL PROCEED AS USUAL AT 9:30AM. = (302/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: MUKUL BAKSHI VS. WORLD AFFAIRS COUNCIL OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 16, 2012, Line 4. DEFENDANT WORLD AFFAIRS COUNCIL OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action is overruled. The complaint sufficiently pleads liability based on the principle of respondeat superior. Paragraph 30(1) of the Second Amended Complaint alleges that office politics partly motivated the attack. Paragraphs 13-15 and 30(1) support liability based on a ratification theory. The Court overrules the demurrer to the negligence cause of action. Paragraphs 33(3) - 33(6) support the claim that plaintiff failed to exercise reasonable care. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. ALL CONTESTED MATTERS WILL BE HEARD AT 10:30AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. UNCONTESTED MATTERS WILL PROCEED AS USUAL AT 9:30AM. = (302/JJM)
